title: ColdRelay vs Google Workspace — Cold Email Infrastructure Comparison
description: Comparing ColdRelay's purpose-built cold email infrastructure with Google Workspace for outbound campaigns. See pricing, deliverability, setup, and scalability side-by-side.

overview.coldrelay: ColdRelay is a purpose-built cold email infrastructure provider designed exclusively for outbound sales campaigns. It provisions Microsoft 365 mailboxes on dedicated, isolated Azure tenants with dedicated IPs. Pricing starts at $1/mailbox/month (dropping to $0.55 at scale), with 100-150 mailboxes per domain, auto-configured DNS (SPF, DKIM, DMARC), and a 99% inbox placement guarantee backed by a money-back promise. Setup takes 2-4 hours with no warmup wait required.
overview.competitor: Google Workspace is Google's productivity and email suite, used by millions of businesses worldwide. The Business Starter plan begins at $7.20/user/month and includes Gmail, Google Drive, Google Meet, and other collaboration tools. While Gmail offers excellent deliverability for regular business email, Google's terms of service explicitly prohibit using Workspace accounts for bulk outreach or cold email campaigns. Accounts used for cold email risk suspension.

featureComparison.coldrelay: Purpose-built for cold email outbound — every feature optimized for deliverability at scale
featureComparison.competitor: General-purpose business email and productivity suite — cold email is against ToS

featureComparison.coldrelay: Dedicated IPs included with isolated Azure tenants for reputation protection
featureComparison.competitor: Shared IP infrastructure — your reputation is tied to Google's pool

featureComparison.coldrelay: Fully automated SPF, DKIM, DMARC setup — ready in minutes
featureComparison.competitor: Manual DNS configuration required for each domain

featureComparison.coldrelay: 100-150 mailboxes per domain — maximizing domain utilization
featureComparison.competitor: No technical limit, but using many mailboxes per domain for cold email violates ToS

featureComparison.coldrelay: Explicitly built for cold outreach — it's the entire product
featureComparison.competitor: Prohibited — Google's Acceptable Use Policy bans unsolicited bulk email

featureComparison.coldrelay: No suspension risk for cold email — that's what the service is for
featureComparison.competitor: High risk — Google actively detects and suspends accounts used for cold outreach

featureComparison.coldrelay: No warmup wait — send from day 1 with pre-warmed infrastructure
featureComparison.competitor: Warmup strongly recommended, typically 2-4 weeks for new accounts
Minutes for basic setup, but hours/days for DNS, warmup, and proper configuration across many domains

featureComparison.coldrelay: Email infrastructure only — no calendar, docs, or video conferencing
featureComparison.competitor: Full suite: Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Meet, and more

featureComparison.coldrelay: Integrates with major cold email platforms (Instantly, Smartlead, etc.)
featureComparison.competitor: Massive ecosystem — thousands of integrations, Chrome extensions, and third-party apps

sections.coldrelayWins: ColdRelay's advantage over Google Workspace for cold email is fundamental: it's built for the job, while Google Workspace explicitly prohibits it. At $1/mailbox vs. $7.20/user, ColdRelay is 7x cheaper before you even consider the risk factor. Google Workspace accounts used for cold email face suspension — meaning you could lose your entire infrastructure overnight with no recourse. ColdRelay eliminates this risk entirely. The dedicated IPs and isolated Azure tenants mean your sending reputation is yours alone, not shared with millions of other Google users. Auto-configured DNS and no-warmup sending mean you can go from zero to live campaigns in hours, not weeks. For a 100-mailbox operation, you're looking at $100/month on ColdRelay vs. $720/month on Google Workspace — and the Google setup carries constant suspension risk.
sections.competitorWins: Google Workspace is the undisputed leader for business email and productivity. If you need a full office suite — Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Meet — Google Workspace delivers incredible value. The ecosystem is unmatched: thousands of integrations, Chrome extensions, and a familiar interface that requires zero training. For your company's primary business email (the accounts your team uses for legitimate day-to-day communication), Google Workspace is the gold standard. Its deliverability for regular business correspondence is outstanding, and Google's spam filtering is the best in the industry.
sections.whoShouldChooseColdRelay: Choose ColdRelay if you're running cold email campaigns and need dedicated infrastructure that won't get shut down. If you're an agency, sales team, or lead generation operation sending outbound at scale, ColdRelay gives you purpose-built infrastructure at a fraction of Google's cost — without the constant threat of account suspension. Use ColdRelay for your outbound sending accounts and keep Google Workspace for your team's primary business email.
sections.whoShouldChooseCompetitor: Choose Google Workspace for your team's primary email accounts — the ones used for client communication, internal collaboration, and everyday business. Google Workspace is not a cold email tool and shouldn't be used as one. If you only need a few email accounts for legitimate business communication and want the full Google productivity suite, Workspace is the right choice.
sections.verdict: These aren't really competing products — they serve fundamentally different purposes. Google Workspace is the best business email and productivity suite available, but it's not built for cold outreach and will punish you for trying. ColdRelay is purpose-built cold email infrastructure that costs 85% less per mailbox and carries zero suspension risk. The smart play is to use both: Google Workspace for your team's daily operations and ColdRelay for your outbound sending infrastructure.

faqs.question: Can I use Google Workspace for cold email?
faqs.answer: Technically you can, but it violates Google's Acceptable Use Policy. Google actively monitors for bulk outreach patterns and suspends accounts that violate their terms. You risk losing your accounts, data, and reputation with no warning.

faqs.question: Why is ColdRelay so much cheaper than Google Workspace?
faqs.answer: ColdRelay provides email infrastructure only — no calendar, docs, or productivity tools. It's optimized specifically for sending, which means the cost structure is fundamentally different. You're paying for mailboxes, not a full office suite.

faqs.question: Should I replace Google Workspace with ColdRelay?
faqs.answer: No. Use both. Keep Google Workspace for your team's primary business email and daily collaboration. Use ColdRelay for your dedicated cold email sending infrastructure. This separation also protects your primary domain's reputation.

faqs.question: How does ColdRelay's deliverability compare to Gmail?
faqs.answer: ColdRelay provides dedicated IPs and isolated Azure tenants, giving you full control over your sending reputation. Gmail uses shared infrastructure. For cold email specifically, ColdRelay's dedicated setup typically delivers better inbox placement because your reputation isn't affected by other senders.

faqs.question: What happens if my Google Workspace account gets suspended for cold email?
faqs.answer: You lose access to the account and all associated data — emails, contacts, drive files. Google's suspension process for ToS violations is often permanent with limited appeal options. This is why purpose-built infrastructure like ColdRelay is the safer choice for outbound.
